sutta: once the exalted 1 while wandering for arms with a great company of monks among the kosalis came to the brahmin village called icha nanggala belonging to them and there he dwelt at ichanangalan wood now the brahmin householders of ichan nanggala heard the news it is said that master gotama the sakyan a wanderer from the sakin clan has arrived at ichan angala and dwells in the wood nearby and of that same master gotama this good repute is noised abroad he is the exalted 1 buddha etc well indeed it is to see such arahants so at the end of that night the brahmin householders of ichanangala taking with them much hard and soft food went to each and angela wood and waited outside the gateway making there a great din and now at that time the venerable nageeta was the personal attendant of the exalted 1 then the exalted 1 called the venerable nageeta and said who are these nageeta that make this great din an uproar just like fisher folk it seems with a great hall of fish lord these are the ichanangala brahmin householders who wait outside the gateway they have brought much hard and soft food for the use of the exalted 1 and the monks of the sangha i have not to do with homage nageeta nor has homage anything to do with me whosoever cannot obtain and will easily and without difficulty this happiness of renunciation this happiness of seclusion this happiness of calm this happiness of awakening which i can obtain at will easily and without difficulty let him enjoy that midden or downhill of happiness that down like happiness that happiness cotton of gains favors and flattery lord let the exhaust exalted 1 accept their offering let the welfare accept now lord is the time for the blessed 1 to accept for wheresoever the exalted 1 shall now go the brahmin householders of town and country will be just as inclined to give lord this is when the sky diva rains big drops the water flows with the slope of the ground even so lord wheresoever the exalted 1 shall now go the brahmin householders of town and country will be bent on making offerings and wise then it is lord because of the virtue wisdom and knowledge of the exalted 1 i have not to do with homies nageeta nor has homage anything to do with me whosoever cannot obtain that widow at this happiness of renunciation seclusion calm and awakening which i can obtain let him enjoy that midden or downhill of happiness that dung like happiness that happiness gotten of gains favors and flattery some devas nageeta cannot obtain at will this happiness which i can obtain stop here for a moment to commend this vulnerable nagita before the buddha took venable ananda as his personal assistant he had other assistance and vulnerable nagita was 1 of them but they did not stay long as the buddha's assistant only later when the buddha chose the variable ananda and the member ananda was so devoted to the buddha that he kept on as abandoner for the last i think about 25 or 20 or 25 years of the buddha's life so you find up to here that because these brahmin householders came with offerings but they made a lot of noise and buddha was not happy the buddha didn't want to see them and this shows this is a part of the buddha's personality and his character he didn't like noise and some books like to comment that the buddha was so perfect that he did not show any personal personal office is like his past personal traits and characters but in the sutas we find it is not quite so on the buddha still has certain characteristics and his traits of character now in 1 of the previous uttasa we had heard that the buddha said that before his enlightenment he had 5 dreams 1 of the dreams was that he walked on a hill of dang meaning he likened the offerings of the requisites to down or excrement not to be soiled by them so in the same way now the buddha said that he was not eager to accept the offerings of the brahmin householders and he said if anybody who is eager to accept that type of happiness which is to him like that like happiness he could they could do so but the buddha said he enjoyed the happiness of seclusion the happiness of renunciation happiness of calm the happiness of awakening and this refers to the bliss of jhana the bliss of jhana is called the happiness or the bliss of seclusion the bliss of renunciation because for example before a person can enter the first jhana he has to be secluded or aloof from sense desires he has to be secluded or aloof from unwholesome states then only he can enter the jana so uh here as in some other sutas and the buddha encouraged indulgence uh in january he did not find anything wrong with them with the indulgence in january blessings in fact in the sutta the buddha said there are 4 advantages to be gotten from indulging in the happiness of jhana namely the fruit of suta panna sukkada gamin anagamin and the 4 stages of fruition of aryan attainment now the buddha continues when you are assembled and met together and live enjoying company i think thus surely these worthies cannot obtain at will easily and without trouble this happiness of renunciation is happiness of seclusion this happiness of calm of awakening which i can obtain so these wordies meet and come together and live and join company or else i see monks joking and making merry by poking 1 another with their fingers then i think surely these worthies cannot obtain at will this happiness of renunciation etc which i can obtain since they joke and make merry by poking 1 another with their fingers or else i see monks after eating as much as their bellies will whole giving themselves over to the delight of bed to the delight of anger to the delight of torpor then i think surely these were these cannot obtain at will easily this happiness of renunciation etc which i can obtain so they cram their bellies to the up at the most and give themselves over to the delight of bed length and torpor or i see among living on the outskirts of some village seated and composed and i think some palm servant or novice will presently disturb this reference and will oust him from that concentration so i am not pleased nagita with that monk's aboard lying on the outskirts of a village then i see a forest dwelling monk seated nodding in a forest and then i think presently when this reverence has got rid of this sleepiness this lethargy he will surely ponder on loneliness making the forest his field of perception so i am pleased nagita by that monk's forest aboard well i see a forest dwelling monk seated uncomposed in a forest and i think presently this reverencer will compose his uncomposed mind or will preserve a composed state of mind so i am pleased navita with that monk's forest aboard oh i see nagita a forest dwelling monk seated composed in a forest and i think presently this reverence will free the unfreed mind i will preserve a state of mind which is free so i am pleased nagita with that monks for us aboard what time nadita i reach the high road and see no 1 either in front or behind me i have leisure even for calls of nature as the end of the sutta
commentary: so in the the second this last part of the suta the buddha said sometimes he sees his hmong disciples enjoying company like like to sit down and talk with each other or make marry by poking 1 another with their fingers or eating as much as they can and going to sleep after the meal and then he said no surely these monks cannot enjoy the happiness of seclusion uh the happiness of renunciation in other words the charming bliss otherwise they won't be behaving like this because the 1 who can obtain the jhanas spends to tends to spend more time in the bliss of jannah like the buddha and his arahant disciples i mean in the sutas we find the buddha and his arahant disciples spend most of the days in jannah absorbed in jhana and the buddha found no fall in that then the other part the buddha talk about some some manga staying in some park and he can be reached by people and if people come and visit him and disturb his concentration then the buddha is not pleased with that that that the bone of the monk and [Music] whereas the type of abode that the monk that the buddha approved is shown is seen here to be the like a really secluded place the forest or cave etc where nobody is likely to reach the monk then the buddha approves of this type of total isolation but it's not used it is not easier for a monk to get used to staying alone [Music] and so the buddha allowed the monasteries to be built for monks to stay together and those monks from there who progressed and wanted to live in seclusion and then he encouraged the monks to go off and live alone in seclusion and once among has gotten used to living alone and seclusion he would prefer to stay in seclusion as a different type of life in exeter's
